{"step_by_step_thinking": "The Couinaud classification divides the liver into 8 segments based on vascularization, bile duct distribution, and lymphatic drainage. The hepatic veins run between adjacent segments and drain into the inferior vena cava. The portal vein divides segments horizontally into superior and inferior sections. The bile ducts are responsible for the vertical division of the segments. Therefore, the structure that differentiates the segments anatomically is the bile duct.", "answer_choice": "C"}
